what: Critical design constraint: single-BGP SPARQL discipline
title: AGENTS.md
text: librdf C library rdflib wraps serious correctness performance bugs conjunctive triple patterns graph’s scale. Multi-pattern SPARQL queries: Joins via shared variables (?s P1 ?o1. ?s P2 ?o2) hang many minutes. Multi-property selects single subject silently return zero rows. Single basic graph patterns (one triple match per query) execute correctly milliseconds. package’s design discipline therefore: SPARQL queries single basic graph patterns. Joins, multi-property assembly, aggregation happen R via dplyr. implemented R/sparql-helpers.R. two primitives (sparql_pairs, sparql_subjects) issue exactly one triple match; four domain helpers (framework_metadata, role_framework_bindings, element_framework_bindings, role_element_bindings) compose multiple single-BGP calls join R. , agent, asked write multi-pattern SPARQL query package’s graphs: don’t. Decompose single-BGP calls join dplyr. future v0.2+ release may add Apache Jena Fuseki backend lifts constraint.

title: Adding a New Framework to cybedtools
text: scripts/020-assemble-jsonld.R, add assembler function register . assembler chooses one two parent-unit constructors depending whether framework workforce-shaped: Use build_role_node() (delegates build_organizing_unit_node(is_role = TRUE)) framework’s parent units work roles work profiles. NICE, DCWF, ENISA ECSF . Use build_organizing_unit_node(is_role = FALSE) parent units something else (skills, learning standards clusters, knowledge areas, competence areas). SFIA, Cyber.org K-12, CSTA, CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2 . paths assert cybed:OrganizingUnit cross-framework queries reach eight frameworks via abstract type. build_role_node() additionally asserts cybed:Role, restricting workforce-queries appropriately. example assumes Framework X workforce-shaped: Framework X non-workforce (enumerates skills, learning standards clusters, competence areas rather roles), replace build_role_node() call : Pick framework_subtype names unit framework’s terminology rather coercing “WorkRole.” See namespace-architecture article existing per-framework subtype table; new frameworks follow pattern. assembly_config$frameworks, add "frameworkx" loop picks .
code: assemble_frameworkx <- function() {   # Read the manifest and tidy CSVs your ingestion script wrote.   prov  <- load_framework_provenance("frameworkx")   roles <- read_framework_table("frameworkx", "roles")   elements <- read_framework_table("frameworkx", "elements")    # Build the framework-level JSON-LD node. Subclasses cybed:Framework via   # the fx: prefix; downstream SPARQL queries match on cybed:Framework   # and so include this framework automatically.   framework_node <- build_framework_node(     framework_id     = "frameworkx-v1",     framework_name   = prov$framework_version,     framework_prefix = "fx",     version          = prov$framework_version,     publisher        = prov$source$publisher,     jurisdiction     = "US",     sector           = "civilian",     specificity      = "cybersecurity-specific",     license          = prov$licensing$source_license,     date_published   = prov$framework_date   )    # One JSON-LD node per work role. build_role_node asserts fx:WorkRole +   # cybed:Role + cybed:OrganizingUnit on each.   role_nodes <- roles |>     purrr::pmap(function(role_id, role_name, ...) {       build_role_node(         role_id              = role_id,         role_name            = role_name,         framework_prefix     = "fx",         framework_role_type  = "WorkRole",         framework_id         = "frameworkx-v1"       )     })    # One JSON-LD node per atomic element. Each subclasses cybed:RoleElement   # so cross-framework SPARQL queries pick them up alongside NICE tasks,   # SFIA skill levels, CSEC2017 essentials, and the rest.   element_nodes <- elements |>     purrr::pmap(function(element_id, text, ...) {       build_role_element_node(         element_id             = element_id,         framework_prefix       = "fx",         framework_element_type = "Element",         element_text           = text,         framework_id           = "frameworkx-v1"       )     })    # The orchestrator collects framework + role + element nodes and the   # prefix; it then assembles the per-framework JSON-LD document and   # merges it into the combined graph.   list(framework = framework_node, roles = role_nodes, elements = element_nodes,        prefix = "fx") }  # Register the assembler so the orchestrator can find it. framework_assemblers[["frameworkx"]] <- assemble_frameworkx framework_to_prefix[["frameworkx"]]  <- "fx" build_organizing_unit_node(   unit_id           = unit_id,   unit_name         = unit_name,   framework_prefix  = "fx",   framework_subtype = "Skill",   # or "StandardGroup", "KnowledgeArea", etc.   is_role           = FALSE,   framework_id      = "frameworkx-v1" )

what: Recipe: pairwise comparison between two frameworks
title: Cross-framework Analysis with cybedtools
code: # Compare per-unit density: NICE (role-first specification) vs SFIA # (skill-first). NICE asserts cybed:Role on its work roles; SFIA asserts # cybed:OrganizingUnit on its skills (skills are not roles in the # workforce-framework sense). The cross-framework cut therefore queries # cybed:OrganizingUnit, which reaches both. ofb <- organizing_unit_framework_bindings(rdf) reb <- role_element_bindings(rdf)  # Filter to the two frameworks of interest, attach element counts to each # unit, then aggregate per-framework statistics. Useful when arguing # about how differently the two structure their specifications. ofb |>   filter(framework_name %in% c("NICE v2 (NIST SP 800-181 Rev 1 components)", "SFIA 9")) |>   left_join(reb |> count(role, name = "element_count"),             by = c("unit" = "role")) |>   group_by(framework_name) |>   summarize(     organizing_unit_count = n(),     mean_elements         = round(mean(element_count, na.rm = TRUE), 1),     median_elements       = median(element_count, na.rm = TRUE),     max_elements          = max(element_count, na.rm = TRUE)   )

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every framework must provenance.yml recording: Framework name version. Source publisher, type, retrieval URL (manual acquisition note). SHA256 canonical source artifact (XLSX, JSON, SQLite, PDF). File size bytes. Retrieval date (ISO-8601) retriever identity (script path). Licensing terms redistribution constraints. Check: provenance.yml exists, parses YAML, contains required keys, recorded SHA256 matches file disk.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every framework must declare expected entity counts docs/framework-invariants.yml. Actual extraction counts must fall within tolerance band. Example: Check: Every ingested framework expected counts declared. Actual counts fall inside declared ranges. Tolerance bands justified inline comments. Rule: actual counts fall outside expected ranges, ingestion flagged potential source revision rather silently accepted. Human review updates expected bounds.
code: nice:   version: "v2"   expected:     work_roles: [41, 41]     unique_tks: [2109, 2113] sfia:   version: "SFIA 9"   expected:     skills: [145, 149]     skill_levels: [670, 675]

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Every within-framework identifier reference must resolve. Examples: DCWF per-role sheets reference Task KSA IDs must exist master catalog. ECSF e-competence references must match known e-CF code pattern. SFIA SkillLevel rows reference Skill codes must exist Skill table (enforced foreign keys SQLite, verified post-extraction). NICE role-TKS associations reference work_role_id (must exist work-roles.csv) statement_id (must exist tasks.csv, knowledge.csv, skills.csv). Check: declared cross-reference, unresolved references enumerated reported. Zero unresolved pass criterion. Current implementation status: Full referential-integrity automation progress. Partial coverage exists via foreign-key constraints source format (SFIA SQLite). Remaining gaps caught implicitly extraction-count bounds (catch structural drift) SPARQL queries (silently drop unresolved references, graph broken refs shows visible element-count shortfalls query results). full explicit check roadmap.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Statement text must pass: UTF-8 validity. replacement characters (U+FFFD), unexpected encoding artifacts. Non-empty. Empty strings element-text columns errors, data. Length sanity. Statements approximately 10 characters approximately 5,000 characters flagged manual review (likely extraction errors merged cells). truncation suspicious round numbers. every statement happens exactly 255 characters, indicates silent column truncation upstream. Check: flagged conditions produce error output. Nothing silently accepted.

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Within framework, every element role ID unique. Across frameworks, element IDs must namespaced framework prefix (e.g., nice:OG-WRL-015-T01, bare T01). JSON-LD assembly enforces . verification rig confirms . Check: duplicate IDs within framework. IDs assembled graphs carry framework prefix.

title: Data integrity protocol

Deterministic row ordering (sort stable key write). Fixed locale number date formatting. Check: Run ingestion twice diff outputs. Zero delta pass criterion. (Exception: provenance.yml retrieval_date differs excluded diff.)

title: Data integrity protocol
text: Hard failures (block downstream work): Missing invalid provenance.yml. SHA256 mismatch source file. Unresolved within-framework references. Duplicate IDs. Invalid UTF-8 empty required text fields. Soft flags (warn allow, written justification): Extraction count outside expected range (indicates source revision, human review required next run). Statements outside length sanity band (likely merged cells truncation, human review).

title: Data integrity protocol
text: verification fails, response never quietly adjust data pass. response : Investigate root cause. Update protocol (invariant wrong) fix source/extraction (data wrong). Document fix audit log human sign-. Re-run.

title: Getting Started with cybedtools

Three semantic abstractions carry work: cybed:Framework: competency learning framework. cybed:OrganizingUnit: framework’s top-level enumerated unit (work role, work profile, skill, grade-band concept, level-concept bucket, Knowledge Area, competence area). cross-framework abstract; queries reach eight frameworks. Workforce frameworks unit genuinely work role profile (NICE / DCWF / ENISA ECSF) additionally subclass cybed:Role. cybed:RoleElement: codable atomic statement attached organizing unit (task, knowledge statement, skill statement, learning standard). Two specialized subtypes parsed sub-content: cybed:Subpoint framework--specified enumerations, cybed:Example pedagogical-scaffolding fragments lifted “Clarification statement:” prose. single SPARQL query targeting cybed:RoleElement returns atomic elements across every framework one pass; targeting cybed:OrganizingUnit returns parents.

title: JSON-LD namespace architecture
text: architecture uses two-tier namespace structure: base vocabulary cross-framework, framework-specific vocabularies subclass base. tier distinct prefix. base vocabulary subsumes conceptual common ground -scope frameworks: framework, organizing unit (framework’s top-level enumerated unit, whether work role, skill, learning standard cluster, knowledge area, competence area), role element (task, knowledge statement, skill statement, competence, learning standard), sub-point, example, element text, source reference, structural metadata (jurisdiction, sector, specificity). Per-framework vocabularies specialize via subclassing, single SPARQL query targeting cybed:OrganizingUnit returns parent units across eight frameworks one pass. Schema.org (schema:) SKOS (skos:) provide outermost vocabulary layer generic properties name, description, prefLabel, altLabel. cybed:OrganizingUnit subClassOf skos:Concept downstream interoperability wider linked-data ecosystem. project namespace URI https://w3id.org/cybed/ontology# (prefix cybed). w3id.org provides stable, redirectable URIs vocabularies via community-maintained GitHub repository, means URI persists across changes maintainer’s personal infrastructure. Resolving URI follows redirect maintained cybed entry w3id.org configuration repo.

what: Why a custom URI rather than reusing existing ontologies
title: JSON-LD namespace architecture
text: natural semantic-web question : mint cybed: rather reuse existing competency skills ontology? Three constraints made reuse insufficient. Coverage. Existing competency ontologies (ESCO, O*NET, CEDEFOP qualifications taxonomies) cover labor-market competencies general terms represent structural commitments specific frameworks package handles. NICE work roles, SFIA skill levels, ENISA ECSF role profiles carry framework-specific structural metadata (TKS classifications, responsibility levels, cross-framework references) ESCO-based mapping either lose distort. Pedagogical-frameworks gap. ESCO workforce-competency ontologies represent K-12 higher-education curricular frameworks (Cyber.org K-12 standards, CSTA standards, CSEC2017 guidelines, DigComp). package’s design commitment make workforce frameworks pedagogical frameworks queryable schema. requires base vocabulary subsumes , existing competency ontologies . Comparison-layer purpose. cybed: schema designed specifically cross-framework comparison: single SPARQL query targeting cybed:OrganizingUnit returns parent units across every framework one pass, regardless whether unit NICE work role, SFIA skill, Cyber.org K-12 grade-band concept, DigComp competence area. Existing ontologies built subsume heterogeneity comparison layer. existing vocabulary fits cleanly, schema reuses : Schema.org (schema:) generic properties (name, description, version, publisher, datePublished, license) SKOS (skos:) prefLabel altLabel. Tier 1 cybed: types specialize framework structure requires . Tier 2 framework prefixes specialize cybed: types framework vocabulary requires .

title: SPARQL query strategy
text: librdf C library rdflib wraps exhibits poor performance silent zero-row results conjunctive triple patterns graph’s scale. Multi-pattern SPARQL joins via shared variables hang many minutes. Multi-property selects single subject silently return rows. Single basic graph patterns (one triple match per SPARQL call) execute fast correctly. package’s discipline therefore: SPARQL queries single basic graph patterns. One triple match per call. Joins, multi-property assembly, aggregation happen R via dplyr. implemented helpers R/sparql-helpers.R: sparql_pairs(rdf, predicate) returns subject-object pairs triples given predicate. sparql_subjects(rdf, predicate, object) returns subjects triples whose predicate object fixed. Domain-level helpers compose primitives: framework_metadata(rdf) returns tibble (framework, name, jurisdiction, sector, specificity). organizing_unit_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (unit, unit_name, framework, framework_name) every framework’s top-level enumerated unit. cross-framework cut. role_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (role, role_name, framework, framework_name) restricted workforce frameworks cybed:Role asserted (NICE / DCWF / ECSF). element_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (element, framework, framework_name) every cybed:RoleElement, including Subpoints Examples. example_framework_bindings(rdf) returns (example, framework, framework_name) cybed:Example subset (Cyber.org K-12 CSTA Clarification scaffolding). role_element_bindings(rdf) returns (role, element) every cybed:hasElement triple. Excludes Examples (reachable via cybed:hasExample). domain helper makes one four single-BGP queries joins results via dplyr left-joins semi-joins.

title: cybedtools
text: Eight cybersecurity workforce learning frameworks. Eight different schemas, eight different vocabularies. Comparing (’s specified , overlap, structural commitments differ) historically meant rebuilding comparison scratch every time, spreadsheet. cybedtools makes comparison one query. package ingests four workforce competency frameworks (NICE, DCWF, SFIA, ENISA ECSF) four pedagogical learning-standards frameworks (Cyber.org K-12, CSTA K-12 CS, ACM/IEEE CSEC2017, JRC DigComp 2.2), expresses shared cybed: semantic schema, exposes small set R helpers let query across one corpus. propose replacement framework attempt re-author framework content. Existing frameworks retain structure vocabulary. package adds comparison layer. ’s : cybersecurity education researchers comparing curricula across frameworks workforce-development analysts mapping job roles training requirements framework authors revisers checking structural coverage peer frameworks doctoral students writing dissertations need cross-framework empirical claims “Top-level units” column reports framework’s top-level enumerated unit. NICE / DCWF / ECSF declare work roles work profiles; SFIA declares skills; CSEC2017 declares Knowledge Areas; DigComp 2.2 declares competence areas. Cyber.org K-12 CSTA name cell groups numbered standards within internal axes (grade band x sub-concept Cyber.org; level x concept CSTA), cybedtools labels StandardGroup. eight types subclass cybed:OrganizingUnit cross-framework queries reach uniformly; NICE / DCWF / ECSF additionally subclass cybed:Role. “Elements” column counts parents, parsed Subpoints, (Cyber.org K-12 CSTA) Clarification-statement Examples; framework_summary carries element_count_strict analyses need count without Examples. title: Construct a cybed:OrganizingUnit node — build_organizing_unit_node
text: Every framework's top-level enumerated unit instance cybed:OrganizingUnit (subClassOf skos:Concept), cross-framework abstract lets one SPARQL query reach eight frameworks' parent units uniformly. Workforce frameworks (NICE, DCWF, ENISA ECSF) unit genuinely work role work profile additionally assert cybed:Role (subClassOf cybed:OrganizingUnit); pass is_role = TRUE . Non-workforce frameworks (SFIA enumerates skills; Cyber.org K-12, CSTA, CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2 enumerate organizing units) assert cybed:OrganizingUnit . unit also carries per-framework subtype (e.g., nice:WorkRole, sfia:Skill, csta:StandardGroup, cyberorg:StandardGroup). Cross-framework queries target cybed:OrganizingUnit; framework-specific queries target per-framework subtype; workforce-queries target cybed:Role. backward-compatible workforce-construction, see build_role_node().

what: Expand parent element nodes with sub-point and example child nodes — expand_with_subpoints
title: Expand parent element nodes with sub-point and example child nodes — expand_with_subpoints
text: Walks list parent element nodes, parses one's cybed:elementText enumerated child fragments, returns list parents plus newly-minted child nodes. child routed per parsed node_type: node_type == "Subpoint" (framework--specified enumeration "", "including", semicolon-list patterns) becomes cybed:Subpoint node, carries parent's framework subtype, appears default cybed:hasElement traversals. node_type == "Example" (pedagogical scaffolding "Clarification statement:" sources) becomes cybed:Example node, carries framework-native subtype, reachable via parent's cybed:hasExample predicate (Examples excluded default cybed:hasElement collections). Parents emit Example children mutated place add cybed:hasExample triples linking Examples. Parents whose text yields fragments pass unchanged. returned list preserves parent order appends children parents. Child IRIs deterministic: <parent_iri>.sub.<ordinal> Subpoints <parent_iri>.example.<ordinal> Examples.

title: Eight-framework summary tibble — framework_summary
text: tibble 8 rows 11 columns. framework_slug Character. Stable slug used URI tail (e.g., "nice-v2", "sfia-9"). framework_name Character. Short display name suitable tables prose. framework_type Character. Content-focus classification, one "workforce" "pedagogy". Independent structural cybed:Role vs cybed:OrganizingUnit distinction. jurisdiction Character. One "US", "EU", "global". organizing_unit_count Integer. Distinct top-level enumerated units bound framework via cybed:partOf. NICE / DCWF / ECSF work roles work profiles; SFIA, skills; Cyber.org K-12, grade-band concepts; CSTA, level-concept buckets; CSEC2017, Knowledge Areas; DigComp 2.2, competence areas. Cross-framework comparison via cybed:OrganizingUnit. element_count_strict Integer. Distinct framework--specified elements (parents plus cybed:Subpoint children). Excludes cybed:Example instances. Use column headline density comparisons across frameworks. element_count_with_examples Integer. Distinct elements including cybed:Example instances (Cyber.org K-12 CSTA Clarification-statement scaffolding). frameworks without Examples equals element_count_strict. example_count Integer. Distinct cybed:Example instances. Non-zero Cyber.org K-12 CSTA ; zero six frameworks. elements_per_organizing_unit_strict Numeric. element_count_strict / organizing_unit_count, rounded one decimal. headline density figure used README. elements_per_organizing_unit_with_examples Numeric. element_count_with_examples / organizing_unit_count, rounded one decimal. Used cross-framework-analysis vignette show Examples inflate Cyber.org K-12 CSTA's apparent specification density. license Character. Distribution license published framework owner.

title: Eight-framework summary tibble — framework_summary
text: Two element-count columns surfaced: element_count_strict counts parents plus parsed Subpoints (framework--specified content); element_count_with_examples additionally counts Cyber.org K-12 CSTA Clarification-statement Examples (pedagogical scaffolding, enumerable sub-standards). frameworks emit Examples (NICE, DCWF, ECSF, SFIA, CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2) two columns equal. README headline "density spread" finding uses strict count cross-framework parity; cross-framework-analysis vignette shows columns side--side discusses encoding heterogeneity drives difference. framework_type column denotes content focus (workforce competencies vs educational standards), structural distinction drives cybed:Role assertion. SFIA carries framework_type == "workforce" specifies workforce skills, parent units assert cybed:OrganizingUnit (cybed:Role) SFIA enumerates skills rather work roles. structural questions, query cybed:Role cybed:OrganizingUnit directly.

Use : Verify package installed SPARQL execution works system (helpful troubleshooting librdf system-library issues). Try domain helpers running full pipeline. Develop new helpers queries graph small enough reason hand. real cross-framework analysis, use load_combined_ntriples_graph() graph assembled staged framework sources.

what: Parse subpoints out of a parent element's text — parse_subpoints
title: Parse subpoints out of a parent element's text — parse_subpoints
text: Heuristic regex parser. Extracts enumerated child fragments parent element's prose, returning tibble one row per fragment. Returns empty tibble atomic statements list lift. Two source patterns recognized, row tagged node_type promoted JSON-LD graph: "Clarification statement:" segments (Cyber.org K-12 CSTA convention). teacher-facing pedagogical scaffolding rather framework--specified enumerations. Rows derived pattern carry node_type == "Example" promoted cybed:Example nodes expand_with_subpoints(). "/ including / examples ..." patterns (NICE, SFIA, ECSF, CSEC2017). within-text enumerations framework specifies part parent's normative content. Rows derived pattern carry node_type == "Subpoint" promoted cybed:Subpoint nodes. full algorithm known limitations documented docs/framework-data-sources.md. Per-framework opt-: set CYBED_DISABLE_SUBPOINT_PARSER env var (comma-separated framework slugs) pass slug framework_slug.

Clarification statement: At this level,   focus on examples such as not using common words; using pass phrases;   combining letters, numbers, and symbols." ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   ordinal text                   node_type #>     <int> <chr>                  <chr>     #> 1       1 not using common words Example   #> 2       2 using pass phrases     Example    # "Such as ..." comma list -> Subpoints. title: cybedtools 0.2.0
text: release introduces structural redesign cybed: vocabulary address two defects v0.1.1 documented fix: cybed:Role misapplied non-workforce frameworks (SFIA enumerates skills, roles; CSTA, Cyber.org K-12, CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2 enumerate organizing units), cybed:Subpoint overstated source frameworks specify promoted Cyber.org K-12 CSTA “Clarification statement:” pedagogical scaffolding first-class sub-standards. fixes require breaking changes data model.

text: cybed:OrganizingUnit (subClassOf skos:Concept) cross-framework abstract every framework’s top-level enumerated unit asserts. Cross-framework SPARQL queries target type rather cybed:Role. Adding new framework future release introduces new per-framework subtype change existing cross-framework queries.

title: cybedtools 0.2.0
text: cybed:Role retained subClassOf cybed:OrganizingUnit asserted three workforce frameworks unit genuinely work role work profile: NICE work roles, DCWF work roles, ENISA ECSF profiles. SFIA skills, Cyber.org K-12 grade-band concepts, CSTA level-concept buckets, CSEC2017 Knowledge Areas, DigComp competence areas longer assert cybed:Role. Queries filter ?x cybed:Role return workforce-framework parents v0.2.0; cross-framework cut, switch cybed:OrganizingUnit. Migration snippet:
code: # v0.1.x cross-framework parent query (returned all 8 frameworks # because all parents asserted cybed:Role): SELECT ?p WHERE { ?p a cybed:Role }  # v0.2.0 equivalent (the same eight-framework cut, via the abstract): SELECT ?p WHERE { ?p a cybed:OrganizingUnit } # v0.1.x R helper (returned all 8 frameworks): role_framework_bindings(rdf)  # v0.2.0 equivalent (cross-framework cut): organizing_unit_framework_bindings(rdf)  # v0.2.0 workforce-only cut (returns NICE / DCWF / ECSF only): role_framework_bindings(rdf)

what: New atomic type: cybed:Example (with cybed:hasExample predicate)
title: cybedtools 0.2.0
text: cybed:Example (subClassOf cybed:RoleElement) new sibling cybed:Subpoint content lifted “Clarification statement:” pedagogical scaffolding (Cyber.org K-12 CSTA convention). Examples differ Subpoints three ways: Examples carry framework-native subtype. Cyber.org K-12 Example typed cybed:Example cybed:RoleElement ; also typed cyberorg:Standard. Queries filter per-framework subtype longer pick Examples accident. Examples reachable via parent element’s cybed:hasExample predicate. excluded default cybed:hasElement traversals, role-level “elements” queries remain restricted framework--specified content. Examples carry cybed:elaborates back-pointers. parent owns Example via cybed:hasExample; Example carry converse link. Two source-data shapes route cybed:Example. (1) Cyber.org K-12 stores Clarification statements inline standard text “Clarification statement:” header; v0.1.1 parser extracted cybed:Subpoint, v0.2.0 reroutes cybed:Example. (2) CSTA K-12 CS stores clarifications separate clarification column rather inline; v0.1.x extract column , v0.2.0 reads directly emit one cybed:Example per non-empty clarification (114 new graph nodes). SFIA, NICE, ECSF, CSEC2017 enumerations (“”, “including”, semicolon-list patterns rather Clarification scaffolding) remain typed cybed:Subpoint. Migration snippet:
code: # v0.1.x: a Cyber.org K-12 sub-point was typed as a sub-standard: SELECT ?s WHERE { ?s a cyberorg:Standard }   # picked up Clarification fragments SELECT ?s WHERE { ?s a cybed:Subpoint }      # picked up Clarification fragments  # v0.2.0: the same fragments are typed cybed:Example instead. SELECT ?s WHERE { ?s a cybed:Example }       # all Cyber.org + CSTA Clarifications

title: cybedtools 0.2.0
text: Two cluster-type names renamed v0.1.x’s StandardCluster suffix match framework’s structural axes. Cyber.org K-12 CSTA organize numbered standards cells multi-dimensional axes (grade band x sub-concept Cyber.org K-12; level x concept CSTA), neither framework names cell published documentation. cybedtools therefore uses descriptive StandardGroup label rather coining pedagogy term source frameworks originate: SFIA’s sfia:Skill, CSEC2017’s csec:KnowledgeArea, DigComp’s digcomp:CompetenceArea retain existing names longer subclass cybed:Role; now subclass cybed:OrganizingUnit directly. NICE / DCWF / ECSF parent subtypes unchanged.

Two element-count columns surfaced analyses can choose strict count (parents + Subpoints) inclusive count (parents + Subpoints + Examples). Returns eight frameworks’ parents. example_framework_bindings() — cybed:Example subset element_framework_bindings(). Returns Cyber.org K-12 CSTA Clarification scaffolding.

Examples deliberately excluded collection role-level “elements” queries remain restricted framework--specified content.

title: cybedtools 0.2.0
text: items v0.1.1 NEWS flagged known limitations carrying v0.1.2 addressed release: cybed:Role abstraction longer applied non-workforce frameworks, Cyber.org K-12 / CSTA Clarification fragments longer carry cybed:Subpoint typing framework-native subtypes. version label moved v0.1.2 v0.2.0 changes breaking semver requires minor bumps breaking changes 0.x range.

title: cybedtools 0.1.1
text: Hidden granularity fix. 2026-05-08 audit eight frameworks found several encode pedagogical specification detail prose (“Clarification statement:” segments, “” example lists, semicolon- delimited enumerations) inside cybed:elementText literals rather discrete numbered standards. naive element count -represented actual content density frameworks. v0.1.1 adds uniform sub-point parser (parse_subpoints()) runs every framework’s elementText literals JSON-LD assembly time promotes parsed sub-point first-class child element node. Frameworks parser finds sub-points (NICE, DCWF, DigComp 2.2) unchanged. Affected frameworks gain sub-point children: Cross-framework per-organizing-unit density recomputes ~50x v0.1.0 ~12x v0.1.1. framing also shifts: eight frameworks specify incommensurable units analysis (work role, skill level, competence, standard, Knowledge Area, competence area) reflect different design philosophies. cybedtools comparison layer makes queryable one graph erase structural distinctions. Density figures comparison aids, quality claims. Parser robustness. first iteration parse_subpoints() - extracted multi-sentence elementText (notably SFIA’s responsibility prose) greedily consuming past sentence boundaries. Two refinements landed: sentence-boundary stop (period + whitespace + uppercase) pure-connective filter (drops “”/“”/“” artifacts source- truncated lists). Re-spot-check brought SFIA ~30% false-positive rate 0/10 sampled, CSEC2017 ~33% 0/2. eight frameworks run parser enabled release. New JSON-LD vocabulary. Two terms added cybed: ontology: cybed:Subpoint (cybed:RoleElement subtype tagging parsed sub-points) cybed:elaborates (predicate linking sub-point parent standard). Sub-points retain framework-native subtypes polymorphic queries: Cyber.org K-12 sub-point typed cyberorg:Standard, cybed:Subpoint, cybed:RoleElement simultaneously. New exported helpers R/jsonld-helpers.R: parse_subpoints() heuristic regex parser build_subpoint_node() JSON-LD constructor sub-points expand_with_subpoints() orchestrator walks parent elements emits sub-point children deterministic IRIs (parent.sub.N) extend_role_element_ids() helper role-builder integration Per-framework opt-. environment variable CYBED_DISABLE_SUBPOINT_PARSER (comma-separated framework slugs) disables sub-point parsing listed frameworks. Useful framework steward prefers numbered-counts. Pipeline scripts now load package directly. 020-assemble-jsonld script previously sourced helpers hand-mirrored file path. v0.1.1 uses pkgload::load_all() (running working tree) library(cybedtools) (running installed package). Tests. 52 new unit semantic tests across parser determinism, sub-point IRI stability, type polymorphism, cybed:elaborates traversal, cluster--leaf reachability, JSON-LD round-trip. 111 tests pass release, skips. Known limitations carrying v0.1.2. cybed:Role abstraction structurally coerced frameworks enumerate roles (SFIA enumerates skills; CSTA, Cyber.org K-12, CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2 enumerate organizing units roles). “roles” column framework_summary “framework’s top-level organizing unit, whatever framework calls ” rather count roles workforce sense. v0.1.2 slated redesign split abstraction. See docs/framework-data-sources.md per-framework structural mapping. cybed:Subpoint type overstated Cyber.org K-12 CSTA “Clarification statement:” examples. formally teacher- facing scaffolding describing level--rigor expectation standard, enumerable sub-standards. current schema types sub-standards, queryable slightly overstates framework expects. v0.1.2 introduce distinct cybed:Example type exclude promoted nodes default cybed:hasElement collections.

title: cybedtools 0.1.0
text: Initial public release. Eight cybersecurity workforce learning frameworks (NICE, DCWF, ECSF, SFIA, Cyber.org K-12, CSTA, ACM/IEEE CSEC2017, DigComp 2.2) ingested shared two-tier JSON-LD schema queryable via single-BGP SPARQL R-side joins.
